text: عَلِيُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ بَعْضِ أَصْحَابِهِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) فِي رَجُلٍ كَاتَبَ مَمْلُوكَهُ وَ اشْتَرَطَ عَلَيْهِ أَنَّ مِيرَاثَهُ لَهُ فَرُفِعَ ذَلِكَ إِلَى أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) فَأَبْطَلَ شَرْطَهُ وَ قَالَ شَرْطُ اللَّهِ قَبْلَ شَرْطِكَ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding a man who contracted his slave and stipulated a condition upon him that his inheritance would be for him. So that was raised to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>, so he<sup>asws</sup> invalidated his condition and said: ‘The Stipulated Condition of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> is before the stipulation of your condition’.
